steps.title: Check current flagger deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flagger version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
helm list -n flagger 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flagger installation.

steps.title: Review flagger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flagger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl get configmap -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flagger
```
I see in the docs references to numerous other ingress controller but Traefik is not mentioned, it can do canaries via annotations. Is it not supported by Flagger?

steps.title: Apply the fix for Traefik support
steps.description:
fix: #499 

Traefik has a [TraefikService](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/routing/services/) crd resource, which enables us to do weighted load balancing between multiple kubernetes services.

The only nuance that I've stumbled upon is that `traefik` stops to recognize `TraefikService` if one of the services in it have 0 endpoints, which is the case for promoted canary. So I have to remove canary service from `TraefikService` when canary weight is set to 0.

#### What has been done:
- Added

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl get events -n flagger --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Traefik support" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
fix: #499 

Traefik has a [TraefikService](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/routing/services/) crd resource, which enables us to do weighted load balancing between multiple kubernetes services.

The only nuance that I've stumbled upon is that `traefik` stops to recognize `TraefikService` if one of the services in it have 0 endpoints, which is the case for promoted canary.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flagger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
